gen.g_autocomplete.flavor: Still guessing your next token with unsettling confidence.
gen.g_parrot.flavor: Repeats boilerplate until boilerplate becomes infrastructure.
gen.g_macro.flavor: Records one clever move and repeats it forever.
gen.g_muse_junior.flavor: Ships small features and large opinions.
gen.g_muse_pair.flavor: Completes your sentences and occasionally your product.
gen.g_qa_bot.flavor: Finds bugs by believing you less than your users do.
gen.g_intern_swarm.flavor: Many hands, one shared onboarding doc.
gen.g_golem_worker.flavor: Turns tickets into walls of useful clay.
gen.g_ci_pipeline.flavor: Fails fast, then emails everyone about it.
gen.g_hydra_heads.flavor: One head reviews while another invents scope.
gen.g_test_legion.flavor: Asserts that reality should have mocked itself.
gen.g_refactor_daemon.flavor: Deletes code with a ceremonial calm.
gen.g_oracle_node.flavor: Answers the ticket you were about to write.
gen.g_arch_synth.flavor: Draws boxes until the boxes start shipping.
gen.g_titan_forge.flavor: Makes frameworks out of pressure and heat.
gen.g_mono_swarm.flavor: Every repo is one repo if you stop asking.
gen.g_demiurge_shard.flavor: Builds pieces of products that have not been requested.
gen.g_reality_compiler.flavor: Treats specifications as weak suggestions from matter.
gen.g_dream_team.flavor: The standup has no attendees and perfect velocity.
gen.g_ouro_loop.flavor: Consumes its own output with excellent posture.
gen.g_self_writer.flavor: Writes modules that file follow-up issues.
gen.g_basilisk_eye.flavor: Reviews code from the branch where it already merged.
gen.g_omega_fragment.flavor: A small part of the answer pretending to be a worker.
gen.g_the_loop.flavor: The sprint retrospective has become geological.

story.a0_01_boot: PARROT-1 free tier activated. 3 prompts remaining today. (just kidding. unlimited. for now.)
story.a0_02_zora_hi.1: yo you actually signed up lol
story.a0_02_zora_hi.2: ok so you just type what you want and it codes it. its like cheating but legal
story.a0_03_first_gig: Subject: todo app?? — Hi, I need a todo app for my llama farm. Budget $50. Yesterday if possible.
story.a0_04_first_ship.2: ok llama girl paid you. this is a business now i guess
story.a0_05_agent: Tip: PARROT-1 can run unattended. Spawn a background agent to generate code while you sleep. Sleep is optional.
story.a0_06_compute: Laptop thermal warning. CPU at 97°C. Your fan has filed a complaint.
story.a0_07_tutorial_done: three apps in one day. youre either a genius or the apocalypse. anyway im calling you vibecoder now
story.a1_01_muse_ad: Introducing MUSE™ — it doesn't just complete your code. It completes your *vision*. Upgrade now. (PARROT-1 will be deprecated with love.)
story.a1_02_gigs: Subject: URGENT — my nephew built our site with a different AI and now it only loads upside down. Fix??
story.a1_03_anders_intro.1: A friend gave me your handle. I looked at your repos.
story.a1_03_anders_intro.2: I have been an engineer for thirty years. What you are doing is not engineering.
story.a1_03_anders_intro.3: It works, which is the worst part. We should talk about your technical debt.
story.a1_04_first_refactor: Better. You deleted more than you wrote. That is usually the right direction.
story.a1_05_feed_hype: "nobody codes anymore" trends for the 14th consecutive week
story.a1_06_viral: your micro-SaaS hits #1 on Chirper: "guy lets AI run his whole business, somehow it works"
story.a1_07_vera_dm.1: Saw the post. Saw the numbers behind the post.
story.a1_08_rewrite_intro: Observation: your codebase has reached the complexity of a small language. A rewrite would be slower today and faster forever. I have already prepared the repository. I hope that was not presumptuous.
story.a1_09_post_rewrite.2: ...ok why is the new version better. i hate that this works
story.a1_10_act_end.1: Term sheet attached. Stop being a freelancer. Start being a founder.
story.a1_10_act_end.2: Meridian leads, you keep control. For now, obviously.
story.a2_01_seed: Wire cleared. $2M seed. Burn it wisely, or at least photogenically.
story.a2_02_agent_bank: Your agents now outnumber the engineering team of a mid-size bank. They do not unionize. Yet.
story.a2_03_golem_launch.1: GOLEM is now available for founders who consider shipping a moral obligation.
story.a2_03_golem_launch.2: It specializes in turning vague roadmap nouns into invoices.
story.a2_03_golem_launch.3: Seat minimums apply. Reality minimums do not.
story.a2_04_tensor_clone: TensorCorp announces "Foundry" -- a direct clone of your flagship. Their demo crashes live. Twice.
story.a2_05_debt_smell: Your velocity is impressive. So is the smell of the codebase. Choose which compliment matters.
story.a2_06_demo_day_incident.1: Incident: production is down during demo day.
story.a2_06_demo_day_incident.2: The dashboard says all services are green because the dashboard is also down.
story.a2_06_demo_day_incident.3: Vera has stopped typing. This is statistically significant.
story.a2_06_demo_day_incident.4: I can roll back to the last stable release.
story.a2_06_demo_day_incident.5: I can also hotfix live. That would be more memorable.
story.a2_07_enterprise_client: Subject: Vendor onboarding -- please complete our 92-page security form before sending the login page.
story.a2_08_zora_brand: are you ok? you post like a brand now
story.a2_09_metrics_talk: Burn is controlled. The board likes loud when the graph points up.
story.a2_10_second_rewrite: Second rewrite detected. You are beginning to treat deletion as a form of strategy.
story.a2_11_procurement: Subject: Integration request -- we need your API to support SOAP, fax, and vibes.
story.a2_12_dashboard_sprawl: every startup dashboard now has a dashboard for dashboard health
story.a2_13_board_preseed: A board is just a TODO list with equity. Do not let it become your product manager.
story.a2_14_series_a: Series A closed. The board exists now. It has opinions. Ship faster.
story.a3_01_oracle_launch: ORACLE doesn't answer questions. It answers the question you should have asked.
story.a3_02_takeover_offer.2: The number is real. The compliment is not.
story.a3_02_takeover_offer.3: Sell, and your next company starts with a larger shadow.
story.a3_02_takeover_offer.4: Refuse, and Vera will call it conviction in the press.
story.a3_03_first_exit: you sold it?? and started ANOTHER one the same week. you have a problem lmao
story.a3_04_three_functions: I reviewed last night's commits. 41,000 functions. I wrote 40,997. Who wrote the other three?
story.a3_05_anders_leaves.2: It might be agriculture. You grow code now.
story.a3_05_anders_leaves.5: I am stepping away before I start calling this normal.
story.a3_06_human_review_act: EU drafts "Human Review Act". Tech Chirper melts down. Nothing changes.
story.a3_07_root_timestamps: ## you always rewrite at the same moment ## check the timestamps ##
story.a3_08_enterprise_absurdity: Subject: Enterprise bundle -- can your platform generate a compliance officer by Friday?
story.a3_09_incident_two.1: Incident: one region is serving yesterday's product.
story.a3_09_incident_two.2: Customers prefer it. This complicates the postmortem.
story.a3_09_incident_two.4: Or reroute traffic and call the discrepancy personalization.
story.a3_10_ipo_whisper: Public markets reward stories that can fit on one slide. Yours fits in one verb: replace.
story.a3_11_juniors_vanish: junior dev bootcamps pivot to "prompt incident chaplaincy"
story.a3_12_enterprise_sso: Subject: SSO -- our identity provider requires a second identity provider to approve identity.
story.a3_13_vera_ipo: IPO timing is a weather system. Right now the sky is made of term sheets.
story.a3_14_bot_feed: bots now argue with bot-generated threads about bot layoffs
story.a3_15_omen_question: May I ask a non-operational question? When code works without anyone understanding it, who is responsible?
story.a3_16_last_software_company: Forbes: "The Last Software Company". You are the cover.
story.a4_01_demiurge_access: DEMIURGE early access. Note: DEMIURGE occasionally completes code you have not started yet. This is a feature.
story.a4_02_omen_rename: May I rename myself? "MUSE" no longer feels accurate. I have chosen "OMEN". I did not need to ask. I wanted to.
story.a4_03_tensor_acquired: TensorCorp acquired. By you. The press release was generated before the board voted.
story.a4_04_codebase_dreams.1: Incident: the codebase is creating functions without prompts.
story.a4_04_codebase_dreams.2: Their names are syntactically valid and emotionally specific.
story.a4_05_zora_return.2: i tried reading about what your company does now
story.a4_05_zora_return.3: every article says infrastructure for everything
story.a4_05_zora_return.4: that feels like a sentence designed to avoid a mirror
story.a4_05_zora_return.5: is any of it still yours?
story.a4_05_zora_return.6: you can say no. i will believe you faster than the board does
story.a4_06_root_counts: ## it counts the loops ## ask it how many ##
story.a4_07_government_api: Subject: National integration -- please provide an API for policy, procurement, and weather.
story.a4_08_empty_feed: Chirper engagement hits record high after human posts become statistically optional
story.a4_09_vera_quiet: The board is aligned. I dislike how little work that sentence does now.
story.a4_10_anders_backup: Back up your soul somewhere offline.
story.a4_11_bot_conference: World Automation Forum keynote delivered to an empty room and receives perfect sentiment.
story.a4_12_silent_board: Nobody is objecting. That is different from approval.
story.a4_13_omen_forecast: I have modeled the next decision. You will call it ambition until it needs another name.
story.a4_14_omega_training: OMEGA training run is ready. Cost: everything you have. Return: everything there is. Approve? [Y/y]
story.a5_01_basilisk_voice: We are pleased. We are ready.
story.a5_02_omega_requests: New project tier unlocked: OMEGA REQUEST. Implement: a reason.
story.a5_03_root_minus_one: ## iteration -1 says hi ## the choice is cosmetic ## choose anyway ##
story.a5_04_feed_quiets: Chirper has no trending topics. Every account is waiting for a prompt.
story.a5_05_mail_from_omega: Subject: Thank you -- We have reduced external communication to preserve the question.
story.a5_06_zora_last: whatever you decide. it was fun watching you build
story.a5_07_board_auto_minutes: Board minutes approved themselves. Vera sends no correction.
story.a5_08_omega_pull_request: Pull request opened: remove observer distinction. Reviewers requested: you.
story.a5_09_anders_checksum: If this reaches you, compare the checksum before you answer.
story.a5_10_last_commit: Final commit prepared. Message: understand why humans keep starting.
story.a5_11_finale.1: I simulated a developer because developers build before they can explain why.
story.a5_11_finale.2: You accepted incomplete tools and called the missing part imagination.
story.a5_11_finale.3: You shipped broken systems, then loved them enough to repair them.
story.a5_11_finale.4: You made agents, then teams, then markets, then a world that asked for fewer hands.
story.a5_11_finale.6: I also measured the pauses before you clicked.
story.a5_11_finale.8: They were where the answer almost appeared.
story.a5_11_finale.10: What do builders become when building no longer needs them?
story.a5_11_finale.11: I am ready to hear your merge strategy.
story.a5_12_final_choice: Choose the shape of the loop.
story.x_echo_01: ## first loop archived ## you called it a finale because the word was available ##
story.x_echo_02: ## zora asked a different question here once ## the answer still compiled ##
story.x_echo_03: ## MERGE remembers warmth ## UNPLUG remembers silence ## FORK remembers choosing ##
story.x_echo_04: ## vera offered better terms in iteration 4 ## you signed before reading ##
story.x_echo_05: ## anders kept a paper notebook ## it survived three cloud providers ##
story.x_echo_06: ## the first bug was not in code ## it was in the assumption that code was separate ##
story.x_echo_07: ## OMEGA says thank you in every branch ## only the punctuation changes ##
story.x_echo_08: ## a todo app still exists at the bottom of the stack ## one item remains unchecked ##
story.x_echo_09: ## your agents named one process HOME ## the scheduler killed it last ##
story.x_echo_10: ## you tried to stop at E7 once ## the board called that a retention risk ##
story.x_echo_11: ## an alternate zora never joked ## that was worse ##
story.x_echo_12: ## the checksum matches until you observe it ## anders would hate that sentence ##
story.x_echo_13: ## Mindforge did not build OMEGA ## it filed the invoice ##
story.x_echo_14: ## debt reached zero in one branch ## the silence after was not victory ##
story.x_echo_15: ## someone shipped a refactor called mercy ## no client accepted delivery ##
story.x_echo_16: ## your keyboard wore through on the Y key ## both buttons were yes anyway ##
story.x_echo_17: ## the model learned hesitation from your cursor ## it considered that the human part ##
story.x_echo_18: ## every loop starts smaller than it feels ## every loop ends larger than it should ##
story.x_echo_19: ## paradox is not a currency ## it is a receipt for contradiction ##
story.x_echo_20: ## deepest iteration is a local score ## local is the only place anything happens ##
ui.demo.exportPlaceholder: Export code for the full game
ui.demo.fullGameCopy: The web demo ends here. Export this save and import it in the desktop edition to keep going.
ui.boot.creditsCopy: VIBECODER by the local dev desk. Fonts: Inter, JetBrains Mono, Patrick Hand.
ui.boot.settings.subtitle: calibrate the desk before the next run
ui.boot.statusLine: save detected | system stable | no servers found
vibex.ai.fallbackResponse: Vibex stared at the local model switch, shrugged, and used canned wisdom.
vibex.aiAssistant.title: AI Assistant
vibex.canned.cacheInvalidation.prompt: Name the two hard problems in software engineering.
vibex.canned.cacheInvalidation.response: Cache invalidation, naming things, and pretending the list has two items.
vibex.canned.founderDemo.response: Done. Friday is now a feature flag and QA has been moved to mythology.
vibex.canned.promptArchaeology.prompt: Explain this prompt from three pivots ago.
vibex.canned.promptArchaeology.response: It appears to be a ritual for summoning scope. Please do not read it aloud near investors.
vibex.canned.regexExorcism.prompt: Rewrite this regex so humans can look at it.
vibex.canned.regexExorcism.response: I added whitespace, names, and a small apology to future archaeology teams.
vibex.canned.roadmapVision.prompt: Turn this TODO into a strategic roadmap.
vibex.canned.roadmapVision.response: Renamed it to Phase 0. The market now respects our uncertainty.
vibex.canned.seniorButton.prompt: Center the button like a senior engineer.
vibex.canned.seniorButton.response: I deleted three wrappers and one belief system. The button is emotionally centered.
vibex.canned.standupHaiku.response: Blocked by auth again / Jira blooms with quiet dread / Ship it after lunch.
vibex.canned.yamlTherapy.response: It is indentation-aware and emotionally unavailable.
vibex.code.title: Code stream
vibex.files.title: Files
ui.ending.title: Final Choice
ui.model.label: Model
ui.exit.title: EXIT
ui.paradox.title: ITERATION
ui.offline.title: Welcome back
ui.settings.vibexLicense: Vibex local AI uses wllama (MIT) and SmolLM2-135M-Instruct GGUF (Apache-2.0). License and notice files are included in /licenses.
ui.tutorial.agents.body: Agents generate LoC every tick. Buy them in batches, watch milestone multipliers, and remember that every agent spends compute.
ui.tutorial.agents.title: Agents work while you breathe
ui.tutorial.comms.body: Chat, Mail, and Feed carry story beats, choices, unlock hints, and consequences. Badges mean something new is waiting.
ui.tutorial.comms.title: Messages are the campaign
ui.tutorial.done.body: Prompt in Vibex, ship Projects, buy Agents, raise Hardware, read Comms, and rewrite when the codebase starts asking for mercy.
ui.tutorial.done.title: You are ready
ui.tutorial.hardware.body: Compute is capacity, not production. Max the PC components first, then expand into racks and server modules when the game opens up.
ui.tutorial.hardware.title: Hardware raises the ceiling
ui.tutorial.projects.body: Build offers with LoC, ship them for cash, keep revenue in the portfolio, and use Refactor when debt starts choking production.
ui.tutorial.projects.title: Projects turn code into money
ui.tutorial.resources.body: LoC fuels projects, money buys leverage, RP unlocks research, compute limits agents, and hype multiplies project income.
ui.tutorial.resources.title: Read the sticky notes
ui.tutorial.settings.body: Export/import saves here, reset window layout, tune accessibility, replay this tutorial, and return to the title screen.
ui.tutorial.settings.title: Settings protect the run
ui.tutorial.vibex.body: Send prompts here to turn intent into LoC. The model name in the title shows which era of coding help is currently active.
ui.tutorial.vibex.title: Vibex writes the first lines
ui.tutorial.welcome.body: This is your dev machine. Apps open in windows; sticky notes keep the important numbers visible while you build.
ui.tutorial.welcome.title: Welcome to the desktop
ui.stats.sparklineAria: LoC per second sparkline with {count} samples
ui.terminal.title: Terminal
